lines.speaker: Holoy
lines.text: Are they any good?! They're dwarven engineering at its best!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make one for myself?

lines.speaker: Holoy
lines.text: Well, firstly you'll need to chop yourself some wood, then use a knife on the wood to whittle out a nice crossbow stock like these here.

lines.speaker: Holoy
lines.text: Then get yourself some metal and a hammer and smith yourself some limbs for the bow, mind that you use the right metals and woods though as some wood is too light to use with some metal and vice versa.

lines.speaker: Holoy
lines.text: Wood and Bronze as they're basic materials, Oak and Blurite, Willow and Iron, Steel and Teak, Mithril and Maple, Adamantite and Mahogany and finally Runite and Yew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, so I have my stock and a pair of limbs... what now?

lines.speaker: Holoy
lines.text: Simply take a hammer and smack the limbs firmly onto the stock. You'll then need a string, only they're not the same as normal bows. You'll need to dry some large animal's meat to get sinew, then spin that on a spinning

lines.speaker: Holoy
lines.text: wheel, it's the only thing we've found to be strong enough for a crossbow.

lines.speaker: Holoy
lines.text: Well.. I don't rightly know... us dwarves don't work with magic, we prefer gold and rock. Much more stable. I guess you could ask the humans at the rangers guild to see if they can do something but I don't want

lines.speaker: Holoy
lines.text: You can smith yourself lots of different bolts, don't forget to flight them with feathers like you do arrows though. You can poison any untipped bolt but there's also the option of tipping them with gems then

lines.speaker: Holoy
lines.text: enchanting them with runes. This can have some pretty powerful effects.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh my poor bank, how will I store all those?!

lines.speaker: Holoy
lines.text: Find Hirko in Keldagrim, he also sells crossbow parts and I'm sure he has something you can use to store bolts in.
